result:
**Goal: Add a Live Rule Editor mode where users type simple Python expressions to define custom cellular automaton rules and see them execute on the grid in real time.**

This would be a built-in REPL/editor overlay where you type something like `sum(neighbors) == 3` for birth and `sum(neighbors) in (2,3)` for survival, or more exotic expressions involving `age`, `x`, `y`, `step`, and `random()` — and the grid immediately starts running your custom rule. Include a library of ~10 starter snippets users can load and tweak, plus the ability to save/load custom rules to `~/.life_saves/custom_rules.json`.

**Why this is the right next move:** The project has 94 modes but they're all pre-built. A live rule editor turns passive viewers into active creators — it's the difference between a museum and a workshop. It also pairs perfectly with the existing Evolutionary Playground (adopt evolved rules into the editor for fine-tuning) and Parameter Explorer (explore your own rules' parameter space).

Want me to build it?
